mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy that raises the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compresses valuation multiples, and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative following month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS (mean return >= 0 or proportion positive > 50%).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor income and collapsing consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and consumer prices signal demand-driven inflation that pressures corporate input costs and accelerates monetary tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises MoM and CPIAUCSL rises MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and CPIAUCSL produces a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in industrial production signal collapsing capacity utilization and imminent earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals imminent contraction in construction activity and related durable goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines > 5% over 2 months are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, which drives upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing output and negative operating leverage, which markets price rapidly into lower equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP indicates broad-based demand recovery that supports earnings growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 acceleration is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.
